---
title: "Project 2 Analysis"
author: "Roshan Adhikari"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(corrplot)
```

## Data Import

```{r data-import}
# Data import
# Using relative path assuming the working directory is set to the file's location
menu <- read.csv("menu.csv")
print(head(menu))
str(menu)
```

## Histogram of Calories

```{r histogram}
# Histogram of calories
plot1 <- ggplot(menu, aes(x = Calories)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 50, fill = "orange", color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "Histogram of Calories",
    x = "Calories",
    y = "Frequency"
  )

print(plot1)
```

## Correlation Analysis

```{r correlation}
# Selecting only numeric columns
numeric_menu <- menu %>% select(where(is.numeric))

# Computed correlation matrix
corr_matrix <- cor(numeric_menu, use = "complete.obs")

# Corr Heatmap
corrplot(corr_matrix,
  method = "color", type = "upper",
  tl.cex = 0.6, number.cex = 0.5,
  title = "Correlation Heatmap of Nutritional Features",
  mar = c(0, 0, 2, 0)
)
```

## Top Correlations with Calories

```{r top-correlations}
# Correlations matrix with Calories
cal_corr <- corr_matrix[, "Calories"]

# Remove Calories itself
cal_corr <- cal_corr[names(cal_corr) != "Calories"]

# Descending sort
sorted_corr <- sort(cal_corr, decreasing = TRUE)

# Dropping features with repeated information
is_dv <- grepl("Daily.Value", names(sorted_corr), ignore.case = TRUE)
is_cfat <- names(sorted_corr) == "Calories.from.Fat"
clean_corr <- sorted_corr[!is_dv & !is_cfat]

# Top correlations
print(clean_corr[1:5])

# Negative correlations
neg_corr <- sorted_corr[sorted_corr < 0]
print(neg_corr)
```

## Feature Visualization

```{r visualization}
# Feature selection
features_1c <- c("Saturated.Fat", "Protein")
features_1d <- c("Vitamin.C....Daily.Value.")

# Scatter plot for 1(c)
for (feat in features_1c) {
  print(
    ggplot(menu, aes_string(x = feat, y = "Calories")) +
      geom_point(color = "steelblue") +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "red") +
      labs(
        title = paste("Calories vs", feat),
        x = feat,
        y = "Calories"
      )
  )
}

# Scatter plot for 1(d)
for (feat in features_1d) {
  print(
    ggplot(menu, aes_string(x = feat, y = "Calories")) +
      geom_point(color = "darkgreen") +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "red") +
      labs(
        title = paste("Calories vs", feat),
        x = feat,
        y = "Calories"
      )
  )
}

# Boxplot for 1(c)
for (feat in features_1c) {
  print(
    ggplot(menu, aes_string(y = feat)) +
      geom_boxplot(fill = "orange") +
      labs(
        title = paste("Boxplot of", feat),
        y = feat
      )
  )
}

# Boxplot for 1(d)
for (feat in features_1d) {
  print(
    ggplot(menu, aes_string(y = feat)) +
      geom_boxplot(fill = "lightgreen") +
      labs(
        title = paste("Boxplot of", feat),
        y = feat
      )
  )
}
```

## Outlier Detection and Imputation

```{r outliers}
# Select numeric columns
numeric_menu <- menu[sapply(menu, is.numeric)]

# Compute median and sd
medians <- apply(numeric_menu, 2, median, na.rm = TRUE)
sds <- apply(numeric_menu, 2, sd, na.rm = TRUE)

print("Median values for all numerical features:")
print(medians)

print("Standard Deviations:")
print(sds)

# Copy numeric data
clean_menu <- numeric_menu

# Function to replace outliers with NaN
replace_outliers <- function(x) {
  Q1 <- quantile(x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
  Q3 <- quantile(x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
  IQR_val <- Q3 - Q1

  lower <- Q1 - 1.5 * IQR_val
  upper <- Q3 + 1.5 * IQR_val

  outliers <- (x < lower | x > upper)
  x[outliers] <- NaN

  return(list(values = x, count = sum(outliers)))
}

# Apply to all numeric features
outlier_results <- lapply(clean_menu, replace_outliers)

# Extract cleaned data
clean_menu <- as.data.frame(lapply(outlier_results, function(x) x$values))

# Count NaNs introduced per feature
nan_counts <- sapply(outlier_results, function(x) x$count)
print("NaN counts introduced per feature:")
print(nan_counts)

# Replace NaN with mean
filled_menu <- clean_menu

for (col in names(filled_menu)) {
  mean_val <- mean(filled_menu[[col]], na.rm = TRUE)
  filled_menu[[col]][is.nan(filled_menu[[col]])] <- mean_val
}

# Recomputing median and sd
medians_after <- apply(filled_menu, 2, median)
sds_after <- apply(filled_menu, 2, sd)

print("Median and SD after imputation:")
print("Median:")
print(medians_after)

print("Standard Deviations:")
print(sds_after)
```
